# agentops_schedule (Resource)

A cron schedule that periodically invokes an agent.

## Example Usage

```terraform
resource "agentops_schedule" "nightly" {
  agent_id  = "agent_01hxyz"
  cron_expr = "0 2 * * *"
  timezone  = "UTC"
  input     = jsonencode({ mode = "full" })
}
```

<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->
## Schema

### Required

- `agent_id` (String) ID of the agent this schedule invokes. Changing this forces a new schedule.
- `cron_expr` (String) Cron expression describing when the schedule fires.

### Optional

- `input` (String) Input payload passed to the agent, as a JSON object.
- `is_enabled` (Boolean) Whether the schedule is active. Defaults to `true`.
- `timezone` (String) IANA timezone in which the cron expression is evaluated. Server-assigned when omitted.

### Read-Only

- `created_at` (String) Creation timestamp.
- `description` (String) Free-form description.
- `editable` (Boolean) Whether the schedule can be edited.
- `id` (String) Schedule identifier.
- `last_fired_at` (String) Timestamp of the last time the schedule fired.
- `name` (String) Human-readable schedule name.
- `source` (String) Origin of the schedule.
- `source_id` (String) Identifier of the schedule's source.
- `trigger_type` (String) Type of trigger backing this schedule.
- `updated_at` (String) Last-update timestamp.
